title: Smoky Roasted Ethiopian Walnuts Stew
description: A dense, nutty stew with caramelized red onions, berbere spice, and roasted walnuts, finished with a smoky paprika flare for a comforting Ethiopian dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Toss walnuts with 1 tsp olive oil, smoked paprika, and a pinch of salt. Spread on a sheet pan and roast for 10–12 minutes until fragrant. Remove and set aside.
In a large pot, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium heat. Add sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until caramelized and golden brown, about 15–18 minutes.
Add berbere, cumin, and coriander to the onions, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for another minute to meld flavors.
Deglaze the pot with vegetable broth, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Add the roasted walnuts, lemon juice, and maple syrup (if using). Simmer uncovered for 10 minutes, allowing flavors to meld and the stew to thicken slightly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Adjust sweetness or acidity if desired.
Serve hot, accompanied by injera, rice, or your preferred side.
